Gutenberg / Open Library books | gutenberg.org feeds; OL dumps | PG catalog "freely available" but no explicit license statement located; OL dumps page shows no license, only archive.org ToS link. **UNVERIFIED ŌĆö disqualifying** | dumps 0.5ŌĆō12 GB; heavy subsetting | Good (AuthorŌåÆWorkŌåÆSubject) | Good text; dates/subjects facets | gutenberg id / OL key | Edition |
| 5. Pure synthetic | generator only | No license. N/A | 0 (code only) | Whatever we design | Template text only; no real-data edge cases (Unicode, gaps); zero README credibility | designed | designed |

## Recommendation: Wikidata mineral graph + deterministic synthetic collection layer ("the mineral cabinet")

Thematically self-documenting for **datacrystal** (crystal systems inside a database named datacrystal), the only candidate with explicit public-domain redistribution, and a real graph. Live-verified coverage (WDQS, 2026-06-11): **6,285** mineral species (`P31=Q12089225`); formula P274 90%; crystal system P556 80%; type locality P2695 67% ŌåÆ **1,337** distinct localities, 1,294 with country P17; named-after P138 78%; IMA status P579 ~100%. Weaknesses found by verification: physical numerics are sparse (Mohs 276, streak 639, density 89, color 92) and WD descriptions are one-liners ŌĆö answered by the Specimen layer (dense numerics, generated prose) and an optional CC BY-SA extracts file (1,733 minerals have enwiki articles).

### Acquisition recipe (one-time, scripted, re-runnable)

1. Three WDQS queries (page with `LIMIT 2000 OFFSET n`; label service for en labels): **minerals** ŌĆö `?m wdt:P31 wd:Q12089225` with OPTIONAL P274 formula, P556 crystal system, P579 IMA status, P1088 Mohs, P2695 type locality, en description + `skos:altLabel` aliases; **localities** ŌĆö ids from query 1: label, P17 country, P625 coords; **countries** ŌĆö label, P297 ISO-2.
2. Normalize ŌåÆ msgspec-msgpack snapshot vendored at `/Users/sh/pyrsistance/data/minerals/`: `minerals.msgpack`, `localities.msgpack`, `countries.msgpack`, the three `.sparql` files, `regenerate.py`, `LICENSE` (CC0 notice + snapshot date).
3. Optional, separate file so the core snapshot stays pure CC0: Wikipedia REST `/page/summary` intros for the 1,733 sitelinked minerals ŌåÆ `extracts.msgpack` + `ATTRIBUTION.md` (CC BY-SA 4.0).
4. Specimens/events are **never vendored** ŌĆö generated deterministically (`seed=42`) by the same generator the benchmarks use.

### Entity model sketch

`@dc.entity` applies `@dataclass(slots=True, weakref_slot=True)` per DESIGN.md; `dc.Unique` = the ROADMAP-item-4 unique secondary-key marker (name TBD).

```python
import datacrystal as dc
from datacrystal import Lazy
from typing import Annotated
from datetime import date, datetime

@dc.entity
class Country:
    qid: Annotated[str, dc.Unique]            # "Q183"
    name: str
    iso2: str | None = None

@dc.entity
class Locality:
    qid: Annotated[str, dc.Unique]
    name: str
    country: Lazy[Country] | None             # single lazy ref
    type_minerals: list[Lazy["Mineral"]]      # one-to-many (avg 3.2; hot spots: Tsumeb)

@dc.entity
class Mineral:
    qid: Annotated[str, dc.Unique]            # natural key: upsert-by-QID
    name: Annotated[str, dc.Index]
    formula: str | None                       # IMA formula, P274 (90%)
    crystal_system: Annotated[str | None, dc.Index]   # 8 values -> bitmap facet
    ima_status: Annotated[str | None, dc.Index]       # ~6 values
    mohs: Annotated[float | None, dc.Index]   # sparse (276/6285): Optional showcase
    description: Annotated[str, dc.FullText]  # WD description+aliases (+opt. extract)
    type_locality: Lazy[Locality] | None = None       # single lazy ref

@dc.entity
class Specimen:                               # synthetic collection layer
    catalog_no: Annotated[str, dc.Unique]     # "DC-000042"
    mineral: Lazy[Mineral]
    mass_g: Annotated[float, dc.Index]        # dense numeric range queries
    quality: Annotated[str, dc.Index]         # {A,B,C,D}
    acquired: date
    notes: Annotated[str, dc.FullText]        # template prose; later Ōēź2 @Vector fields

@dc.entity(frozen=True)
class CatalogEvent:                           # append-only provenance log
    specimen: Lazy[Specimen]
    kind: Annotated[str, dc.Index]            # acquired/loaned/sold/relabeled
    at: datetime
    note: str
```

Requirement mapping: lazy single refs `Specimen.mineral`, `Mineral.type_locality`; list-of-refs `Locality.type_minerals`; unique index `qid`/`catalog_no`; bitmap facets at ideal cardinalities (8/6/4/~150 country); dense `mass_g` + sparse `mohs`; FTS on `notes`+`description`; future `notes_vec`+`photo_vec` on Specimen satisfies the Ōēź2-@Vector-fields SDA requirement; frozen `CatalogEvent`.

**Counts/size**: vendored 6,285 + 1,337 + ~150 entities Ōēł 1.5ŌĆō2.5 MB msgpack (+ optional extracts Ōēł 1ŌĆō1.5 MB; total < 4 MB; estimates). Demo/test default generates 2,000 Specimen + ~2,400 CatalogEvent ŌåÆ ~12k objects: bitmap indexes visibly beat scans, setup stays sub-second.

## Fallback

Candidate 5 folded into the recommendation: if WDQS snapshotting stalls, ship the **generator only**, seeded with a ~200-mineral embedded vocabulary (mineral names and crystal systems are facts, CC0 anyway) ŌĆö same entity model, same tests, zero license risk; only README "real data" credibility is lost. Chinook (MIT, verified) is the named real-data alternate, but its prose-free fields cannot validate the FTS watermark harness ŌĆö the deciding defect.

## Scaling into the perf generator

The vendored real data **is** the generator's vocabulary, so README demo, integration tests, FTS harness, and benchmarks share one language. The real entities are the fixed low-cardinality backbone (already at natural maximum: ~6.3k minerals, 1.3k localities, ~150 countries); scale comes from the collection layer: specimens sampled Zipf-over-minerals with hot localities (realistic bitmap AND/OR skew), events = 1 `acquired` + Poisson(0.2) extras per specimen. **1M-object preset Ōēł 7.8k real backbone + 450k Specimen + ~540k CatalogEvent.** Knobs: `--specimens`, `--events-mean`, `--seed`; presets `tiny` (~12k, demo/tests), `bench` (1M), `stress` (5M ŌĆö the positioning envelope's edge). The same Condition AST queries run unchanged at every scale (`(Specimen.quality == 'A') & (Mineral.crystal_system == 'cubic')`, `mass_g` ranges); frozen events exercise append-only commit throughput; `notes` templates feed FTS and, later, embedding benchmarks.

perf.summary: Designed datacrystal's v0.x performance validation suite: a 13-row benchmark table where every gate is a same-run ratio (engine vs re-measured floor, big-corpus vs small-corpus) rather than absolute wall-clock, anchored to the feasibility numbers (600 B/obj, 256 ns decode, 20-26 ns/obj scans, 306 us roaring AND) and including the two O() proofs ŌĆö boot O(checkpoint) vs write-history churn and watermark apply O(delta) at fixed delta across 10k vs 1M corpora (the sda_store lesson). A deterministic stdlib-random generator (seed 0xDC, no wall-clock/uuid4) reuses the Person/City/Note/Tag demo vocabulary with frozen append-only Notes, hub fan-in, and slug unique keys at N=10k/100k/1M. Harness is pytest-benchmark (gates as same-run asserts, non-timing metrics via extra_info) run via `uv run --group bench`, with a <2-min PR gate suite at N<=100k, a nightly 1M sweep feeding a github-action-benchmark trend (repo-internal until PyPI reservation), and release comparison against committed tag baselines.
perf.markdown:
# datacrystal v0.x performance validation suite

Principle: every **gate** is a ratio of two quantities measured in the *same run on the same machine* (engine vs floor, big-corpus vs small-corpus, churned vs clean) or a byte count ŌĆö never absolute wall-clock. The feasibility anchors (~600 B/obj, ~256 ns msgspec decode, 20ŌĆō26 ns/obj scan, ~306 ┬Ąs roaring AND/10M) are re-measured each run as "floors"; the engine is gated on its overhead *over* its own floor. Absolute numbers are tracked as **trends** only.

## 1. Benchmark table

| name | guards | metric | threshold form | cadence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `mem_bytes_per_object` | 600 B/obj all-in envelope ŌåÆ the 1ŌĆō5 M-object positioning | (RSS_after ŌłÆ RSS_before)/N, fresh subprocess, N live registered entities (registry+indexes+Lazy) | Ōēż 690 B/obj (anchor +15%); bytes, runner-stable; also ratio vs bare slots-dataclass RSS Ōēż 5├Ś (trend) | PR @100k; nightly @1M |
| `commit_tput_small` | buffer-until-commit storer + 3-phase commit overhead (ADR-001 P1/P2/P3) | obj/s over 100 txns ├Ś 100 dirty objs | Ōēź 1/3 of same-run floor = msgspec-encode + SQLite `executemany` blob insert of identical payloads (engine Ōēż 3├Ś floor) | every PR |
| `commit_tput_large` | large-txn amortization; P1 capture must not be quadratic | obj/s, 1 txn ├Ś N objs | engine Ōēż 2├Ś same floor; t(100k)/t(10k) Ōēż 12 (linear) | PR @10k; nightly @100k |
| `commit_latency_fsync` | honest fsync policy triad; fsync floor never hidden | p50/p99 of 1-obj commit under `never`/`interval`/`per-commit` | t(per-commit) ŌłÆ t(never) within 0.5ŌĆō2├Ś same-run raw `os.fsync` (4 KB) floor; t(never) Ōēż 5├Ś encode floor; p99 trend | every PR |
| `boot_vs_liveset` | boot = O(persisted checkpoint); SQLite B-tree *is* the boot index | cold openŌåÆroot-ready wall time, fresh subprocess, checkpoint of N objs | boot(10┬ĘN)/boot(N) Ōēż 12 (Ōēłlinear in checkpoint, no superlinear) | PR 10k/100k; nightly 1M |
| `boot_vs_history` | **O(checkpoint), never O(write history)** | same 100k live set; history A = clean insert, history B = 20├Ś churn (rewrites+deletes, no GC) | boot(B)/boot(A) Ōēż 1.25 | PR (10k live); nightly (100k) |
| `hydrate_batch` | 256 ns/obj decode anchor; batch hydration API (SDA delta) | ns/obj, load-many of 10k ghost OIDs | Ōēż 4├Ś same-run raw msgspec decode-to-dataclass loop (engine adds Ōēż 3├Ś decode); ns/obj trend | every PR |
| `hydrate_n_plus_1` | "N+1 must never be the user's problem" | batch(1k OIDs) vs loop of 1k single `Lazy.get()` | batch speedup Ōēź 5├Ś | every PR |
| `query_bitmap_vs_scan` | why bitmap indexes exist (scans 20ŌĆō26 ns/obj) | 2-predicate AND (`(Person.city=='Berlin') & (Person.age>=18)`, ~1% selectivity): Condition-AST query (no hydration) vs full Python scan | speedup Ōēź 10├Ś @100k, Ōēź 50├Ś @1M; query Ōēż 20├Ś same-run bare pyroaring AND (306 ┬Ąs anchor); scan ns/obj trend | PR @100k; nightly @1M |
| `unique_key_lookup` | unique secondary-key index (URIs/slugs, upsert-by-natural-key ŌĆö SDA delta) | lookups/s, hit + miss | t(@1M)/t(@10k) Ōēż 2 (ŌēłO(1)); Ōēż 50├Ś same-run plain dict lookup | PR 10k/100k; nightly 1M |
| `watermark_apply_fixed_delta` | **O(delta), never O(corpus)** ŌĆö the sda_store post-mortem; public pipeline contract | apply identical 1k-op delta (bitmap index + FTS5 sidecar once it lands) at two corpus sizes | t(@big)/t(@small) Ōēż 1.2 ŌĆö PR: 10k vs 100k; nightly: 10k vs 1M | every PR + nightly |
| `snapshot_cost` | ADR-001 rider 2: `store.snapshot()` is the cheap pressure valve | time to take watermark view (frozen DTOs/bitmaps) | t(@1M)/t(@100k) Ōēż 3 (sublinear; should be ŌēłO(1)) | nightly |
| `file_size_amplification` | SQLite-blob honesty; churn must not balloon the store | bytes-on-disk / ╬Ż live msgpack payload bytes | Ōēż 1.6 after clean insert; Ōēż 3.0 after 10├Ś churn + housekeeping (VACUUM/`incremental_vacuum` policy) | nightly |

Recovery/crash benches are correctness tests (torn-tail fuzzing), not perf ŌĆö excluded here.

## 2. Synthetic generator

`benchmarks/_domain.py` + `benchmarks/_gen.py`, importable by tests and docs. Fully deterministic: single `random.Random(0xDC)` (stdlib), **no wall-clock, no uuid4, no `os.urandom`** in generated data; timestamps are `epoch + seq` ints; ids are sequence-derived. Same `(seed, N, generator_version)` ŌćÆ byte-identical store, so store files for boot benches are built once per session and cached in tmp keyed by that tuple plus format version.

**Entity mix** (reuses the demo-domain vocabulary already in DESIGN.md/SDA docs ŌĆö `Person.first_name/city/age`, 'Berlin', 'Thomas', slugs-as-unique-keys, frozen event records):
- 70% `Person(slug, first_name, city: Lazy[City], age, manager: Lazy[Person]|None, notes: list[Lazy[Note]], tags)` ŌĆö `slug = f"person/{i:08d}"` feeds `unique_key_lookup`; `age`/`city` distributions engineered so the canonical predicate hits ~1%.
- 20% `Note` with `@entity(frozen=True)` (append-only agent-memory persona; exercises never-arming dirty tracking) ŌåÆ `author: Lazy[Person]`, text from a fixed 5k-word vocabulary (later feeds FTS).
- 8% `Tag`, 2% `City` ŌĆö **hub objects**: cities Ōēł N/500 with zipf-ish fan-in; 10 "mega-tags" referenced by ~10% of persons (worst-case bitmap density).

**Topology knobs**: ref fan-out 0ŌĆō5 notes/person; `manager` chains form org-trees of depth Ōēż 6 (ghost-chain traversal); ~0.1% cycles (personŌåöperson) to keep the cyclic-GC path honest. **Scale knob** N Ōłł {10k, 100k, 1M}. **Generation target**: Ōēź 200k entities/s pure Python (1M Ōēż ~10 s) so setup never dominates; generation time itself is asserted Ōēż 30 s @1M as a tripwire.

## 3. Harness

**pytest-benchmark**, not a custom timer. Justification: calibration, warmup, GC-disabled timing, IQR outlier rejection, `--benchmark-autosave` JSON history, `--benchmark-compare-fail` ŌĆö a custom harness re-implements all of that and bitrots; pytest integration means benches share fixtures with the test suite. Two adaptations: (a) **gates are plain `assert`s on same-run ratios** inside one test (two `benchmark.pedantic`-style timed blocks compared directly) ŌĆö they do not depend on stored JSON; (b) non-timing metrics (RSS bytes, file bytes, computed ratios) are attached via `benchmark.extra_info` so they ride the same JSON history. Subprocess benches (boot, RSS) use a tiny `runpy` child + `resource.getrusage`; the parent test times/records them through the same mechanism.

Local UX (`bench` dependency-group in pyproject; pytest-benchmark + psutil stay out of core deps):

```
uv run --group bench pytest benchmarks -m bench_pr            # fast gate suite
uv run --group bench pytest benchmarks --benchmark-autosave   # full + save history
make bench / make bench-full                                  # aliases of the above
```

Results: `.benchmarks/<machine-id>/*.json` locally (gitignored). CI uploads the JSON as an artifact; nightly appends to a `bench-data` branch consumed by `github-action-benchmark` (dashboard stays repo-internal until the PyPI name is reserved and the repo goes public). Release baselines are committed as `benchmarks/baselines/<tag>.json`.

## 4. CI stability tactics

- **Trend-vs-gate split (the core tactic)**: gates = same-run ratios (engine/floor, big/small corpus, churn/clean), byte budgets, and the two O() proofs (`boot_vs_history`, `watermark_apply_fixed_delta`) ŌĆö these fail the build. Trends = all absolute ns/ops numbers ŌĆö `github-action-benchmark` alerts at 130% regression vs rolling median, never fails a PR.
- **Pinned execution**: `benchmark.pedantic(rounds=20, warmup_rounds=5, iterations=pinned)` ŌĆö no auto-calibration drift between runs; one pinned runner class; pinned CPython 3.14.x patch version (bumped deliberately, with a baseline reset note).
- **GC discipline**: timing with GC disabled (pytest-benchmark default), explicit `gc.collect()` between rounds, `gc.freeze()` after corpus build ŌĆö which also dogfoods the design's own pause mitigation.
- **Subprocess benches**: 5 repeats; *min* for boot (noise is strictly additive), *median* for RSS.
- **Threshold headroom**: every gate ratio is set Ōēź 2├Ś the noise observed over the first two weeks of nightlies before it becomes blocking (gates start as warnings, flip to hard after 14 green nights).
- **Every PR** (`-m bench_pr`, < 2 min): N Ōēż 100k only ŌĆö both O() ratio gates at reduced scale (10k vs 100k), commit tput/latency, hydration pair, bitmap-vs-scan @100k, unique-key @10k/100k, RSS @100k. Store fixtures cached across the job via the deterministic generator key.
- **Nightly** (`-m bench_full`, ~30ŌĆō45 min): full 1M sweep, 10k-vs-1M O(delta)/O(checkpoint) proofs, fsync p99, snapshot cost, file amplification with churn + housekeeping, trend upload.
- **Release**: nightly suite + `--benchmark-compare-fail=median:20%` against the previous release tag's committed baseline, reviewed by hand before tagging.

dataCritique.summary: The dataset recommendation survives the license/data attack: I independently re-verified Wikidata's licensing page ("All structured data in the main, property and lexeme namespaces is made available under the Creative Commons CC0 License"), reproduced the headline count live on WDQS (exactly 6,285 items with P31=Q12089225 "mineral species"), confirmed P2695 = "type locality (geology)", and confirmed the Wikipedia-extracts (CC BY-SA 4.0) and MovieLens ("may not use ... for any commercial or revenue-bearing purposes without first obtaining permission") characterizations ŌĆö and the graph is genuinely not flat (Lazy single refs, list-of-refs, unique keys, frozen entity all real). The damage is elsewhere: (A) and (B) contradict each other about what domain the benchmarks actually run on, (A)'s flagship query is a cross-entity join the ratified v0.x Condition AST cannot execute, and several perf gates measure warm page cache labeled "cold" or compare against floors (1-object encode, pyroaring AND at 100k, two-sided fsync bounds) that are miscalibrated or will flake on shared runners.

defects.defect: (A) and (B) define two different benchmark domains and both claim to be THE generator. (A) 'Scaling into the perf generator' says the vendored mineral data IS the generator vocabulary (1M preset = 7.8k backbone + 450k Specimen + ~540k CatalogEvent, queries on Specimen.quality/mass_g) and that 'README demo, integration tests, FTS harness, and benchmarks share one language'; (B) ┬¦2 defines benchmarks/_domain.py as 70% Person / 20% frozen Note / 8% Tag / 2% City with 'Berlin' predicates, slugs, manager chains depth Ōēż6, and 0.1% cycles. Entity mix, facet cardinalities, ref topology, frozen ratio, and the canonical gate predicates all differ ŌĆö the suite's corpus identity is undefined, and the deep-traversal/cycle topology (B) needs exists in neither vendored data nor (A)'s Specimen layer.
defects.fix: Pick one domain before any code: port (B)'s topology requirements into the mineral-cabinet generator (manager-chain analog: Specimen provenance/exchange chains depth Ōēż6, ~0.1% reference cycles, 10 'mega' minerals as hub fan-in, a ~1%-selectivity engineered predicate on Specimen fields), keep Person/Berlin only as DESIGN.md prose; or explicitly declare the perf domain separate and delete (A)'s 'benchmarks share one language' and 1M-preset paragraph.

defects.defect: (A)'s flagship query `(Specimen.quality == 'A') & (Mineral.crystal_system == 'cubic')` is a cross-entity join. The ratified v0.x query story (DESIGN.md tier 1, ROADMAP item 4) is per-entity-class bitmap indexes + Condition AST returning a bitmap of ids of ONE class; ANDing a Specimen-space bitmap with a Mineral-space bitmap is meaningless, and joins are explicitly tier-2 (Arrow/DuckDB, v1) / DuckPGQ (v1, docs-only). The README/demo centerpiece query cannot run on the first runnable version, and (A) claims it 'runs unchanged at every scale'.
defects.fix: Replace with a single-class predicate (e.g. `(Specimen.quality == 'A') & (Specimen.mass_g >= 100.0)`), or denormalize crystal_system onto Specimen in the demo model; if a semi-join is wanted, spec it honestly as a ref-OID-bitmap predicate (`Specimen.mineral.in_(bitmap_from_mineral_query)`) and get it ratified as a Condition AST extension first.

defects.defect: Boot benchmarks measure warm OS page cache while labeled 'cold openŌåÆroot-ready'. Store files are 'built once per session and cached in tmp', the bench runs in a fresh subprocess (which clears the process, not the page cache), and (B) takes the *min* of 5 repeats ŌĆö systematically selecting the most-cached run. This is exactly 'cached SQLite page reads pretending to be cold boot': the O(checkpoint) envelope is only ever validated in the CPU-bound cached regime, where superlinear cold-I/O effects (B-tree depth, readahead patterns) are invisible.
defects.fix: Rename the metric to warm-cache boot and document it (the ratio gates boot(10N)/boot(N) and boot_vs_history remain mostly valid within that regime), and add a nightly true-cold variant: copy the store file to a fresh inode then evict (Linux CI: `posix_fadvise(POSIX_FADV_DONTNEED)` on the copy or cgroup memory pressure; macOS dev: `purge`), gated as its own same-run ratio pair.

defects.defect: commit_latency_fsync gates will flake or are miscalibrated by construction, on every PR. (1) Two-sided bound: t(per-commit) ŌłÆ t(never) within 0.5ŌĆō2├Ś raw os.fsync(4 KB) ŌĆö a difference of two noisy quantities against a floor that is itself bimodal on shared runners (virtio/overlayfs fsync ranges ~100 ┬Ąs to ~10 ms run-to-run; macOS F_FULLFSYNC is another regime); the 0.5├Ś lower bound fails legitimately if SQLite coalesces journal syncs. (2) t(never) Ōēż 5├Ś 'encode floor': a 1-object msgspec encode is ~sub-┬Ąs while any real 1-object commit carries fixed SQLite BEGIN/COMMIT + lease/watermark bookkeeping in the tens of ┬Ąs ŌĆö permanently red regardless of engine quality. Related: commit_tput_small's floor ('executemany of identical payloads') does not pin transaction-boundary parity (100 separate floor txns vs one batch) or fsync policy parity with the engine run.
defects.fix: Gate only the policy ordering (t(per-commit) > t(interval) Ōēź t(never)) plus a one-sided sanity check (difference Ōēź ~0.3├Ś same-run fsync floor) and move it to the nightly pinned runner; define t(never)'s floor as encode + a real 1-row SQLite transaction (BEGIN/INSERT/COMMIT, synchronous=OFF); write floor-parity rules into the suite spec: every floor replicates the engine run's transaction boundaries and fsync policy exactly.

defects.defect: query_bitmap_vs_scan's 'query Ōēż 20├Ś same-run bare pyroaring AND' transplants a constant calibrated at the 306 ┬Ąs/10M-id anchor to PR scale (100k), where the bare AND floor shrinks to ~1ŌĆō5 ┬Ąs but the engine's fixed per-query Python overhead (AST walk, index lookups, result wrapping ŌĆö tens of ┬Ąs) does not shrink with N. The ratio at 100k lands anywhere between ~10├Ś and ~100├Ś depending on constant overhead, so the gate fails or passes on fixed cost, not regression ŌĆö likely permanently red at PR scale or tuned into meaninglessness by the 2├Ś-noise headroom rule.
defects.fix: Keep the floor-ratio gate only at 1M nightly; at PR scale gate the absolute overhead (t_query ŌłÆ t_bare_AND, same run) against a fixed per-query budget (e.g. Ōēż 100 ┬Ąs) or track it as trend-only, alongside the already-sound scan-speedup ratio gates (Ōēź10├Ś/Ōēź50├Ś).

defects.defect: watermark_apply_fixed_delta's t(big)/t(small) Ōēż 1.2 will spike non-deterministically once the FTS5 sidecar rides the delta ('once it lands'): FTS5 automerge triggers on cumulative segment history, so one 1k-op delta at the 1M corpus can absorb a multi-level segment merge that the 10k corpus never sees ŌĆö blowing 1.2 on a randomly-chosen run of the suite's single most load-bearing O() proof, which also means the 14-green-nights warning-to-gate promotion will stall on it.
defects.fix: Gate the bitmap-index-only delta path at 1.2; for the FTS5-inclusive path, pin merge behavior (set 'automerge'/'usermerge' explicitly, or run a 'merge' to quiescence before timing) and gate the median over Ōēź10 consecutive deltas instead of one.

defects.defect: The gating mechanism as written ŌĆö 'two benchmark.pedantic-style timed blocks compared directly' inside one test ŌĆö is not implementable with pytest-benchmark: the benchmark fixture raises FixtureAlreadyUsed on second use per test, so every same-run engine/floor ratio needs a hand-rolled timer for the floor side anyway, quietly contradicting the 'pytest-benchmark, not a custom timer' justification and leaving floor timing without the calibration/GC-off/outlier treatment the engine side gets.
defects.fix: Spec it explicitly: engine side rides the benchmark fixture (trend JSON); floors are timed by one small shared helper (perf_counter loop with gc disabled, same rounds/warmup constants as the pedantic config) provided as a session fixture, and the ratio assert lives in the test body.

defects.defect: 'Same (seed, N, generator_version) ŌćÆ byte-identical store' is an unproven overclaim with hidden non-determinism sources unaddressed: (a) DESIGN.md ports EclipseStore's partitioned TID space, and EclipseStore TIDs are wall-clock-derived ŌĆö if datacrystal TIDs carry timestamps, store bytes differ every run; (b) PYTHONHASHSEED is never pinned anywhere in (B), so any set iteration in generation or commit batching breaks byte-identity; (c) SQLite page layout varies across library versions and the cache key (seed, N, generator_version, format version) omits sqlite/python versions.
defects.fix: Downgrade the claim to 'semantically identical' unless TIDs are spec'd sequence-derived; pin PYTHONHASHSEED=0 in the bench environment and add a no-set-iteration rule to the generator; extend the fixture cache key with sqlite3.sqlite_version and the CPython version, and verify with a content hash recorded at first build.

defects.defect: RSS measurement via resource.getrusage cannot implement the stated metric: ru_maxrss is the monotonic high-water mark, so '(RSS_after ŌłÆ RSS_before)/N' either doesn't work at all (before/after deltas of a monotonic counter) or silently reports the build-phase peak (transient encode buffers, generator scratch) instead of steady-state live-graph residency, inflating bytes/object against the 690 B gate; ru_maxrss is also bytes on macOS vs kilobytes on Linux.
defects.fix: Read current RSS (psutil.Process().memory_info().rss ŌĆö psutil is already in the bench group) before and after corpus registration, after gc.collect() and an allocator trim; keep ru_maxrss only as a supplementary peak trend.

defects.defect: The acquisition recipe pages WDQS with 'LIMIT 2000 OFFSET n' and no ORDER BY: SPARQL result order is unspecified without ORDER BY, so pages can overlap or skip rows between requests (especially with the label service and OPTIONALs in the pattern), making regenerate.py able to silently drop or duplicate minerals ŌĆö undermining the 're-runnable' snapshot claim.
defects.fix: Add ORDER BY ?m (and deterministic ordering keys) to all three .sparql files, page on the ordered QID, and have regenerate.py assert the final distinct count against the expected total (currently 6,285) before writing the msgpack files.

defects.defect: The CC BY-SA extracts handling is thinner than the verified attribution requirements: Wikipedia reuse requires per-page credit (hyperlink/URL to each source article), an indicate-changes statement (intro extracts are excerpts, i.e. modifications), and a license notice with each distributed copy. A bare 'ATTRIBUTION.md (CC BY-SA 4.0)' doesn't say this, and nothing specifies whether data/minerals/ ŌĆö including extracts.msgpack ŌĆö ships in the sdist/wheel, which would put share-alike content inside a package whose metadata will presumably declare a single permissive license.
defects.fix: Spec ATTRIBUTION.md to list per-mineral source-article URLs, the CC BY-SA 4.0 link, and an 'excerpted intro paragraphs, otherwise unmodified' changes note; exclude extracts.msgpack from the wheel/sdist (fetch-on-demand via regenerate.py) or explicitly declare the mixed licensing in package metadata before the PyPI name goes live.

defects.defect: (B) violates its own headline principle ('never absolute wall-clock') in ┬¦2: 'generation time itself is asserted Ōēż 30 s @1M as a tripwire' is an absolute wall-clock gate that will flake on contended shared runners (a 3├Ś slowdown over the ~10 s expectation is routine on busy CI), failing nightly runs for reasons unrelated to any regression.
defects.fix: Make the tripwire a same-run ratio against a trivial floor measured in the same process (e.g. generation time Ōēż K├Ś the time to msgspec-encode the same object count), or demote it to a trend alert via extra_info like the other absolute numbers.

fitness.summary: Defined 17 prioritized architectural fitness functions for datacrystal, each tied to a ratified decision (ADR-001, ROADMAP, DESIGN amendments, SDA lessons, Never list) with concrete executable mechanisms, thresholds, gate/trend classification, cadence, and failure actions. Named the "M0 five" that must exist before any engine code (pickle-free AST gate, dependency/import isolation, crash-torture harness, ADR-001 conformance spec, replay-determinism/watermark contract spec) and sketched uv-run local commands plus a three-workflow CI wiring (PR gate / nightly deep / release golden-matrix) with complexity-ratio gating to stay stable on shared runners.
fitness.markdown:
# datacrystal ŌĆö Architectural Fitness Functions (v0 kickoff)

Automated, continuously-evaluated guards on architectural characteristics (Ford/Parsons), derived from the ratified docs. Feature correctness lives in the normal test suite; these exist so the *architecture* cannot silently erode.

## 1. Prioritized catalog

| # | Name | Guards (source) | Mechanism (executable check) | Threshold | Gate/Trend | Cadence | On failure |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | crash-torture | No partial commit ever visible; three-phase commit; "torn-write/kill -9 fuzzing from v0.1" (ADR-001; DESIGN risk 4) | hypothesis `RuleBasedStateMachine` drives store ops in a child process; fault hook in a `FaultyStorage` wrapper of the 3-method protocol injects `os._exit`/short writes at random byte offsets, plus real `SIGKILL` at randomized commit-phase boundaries; reopen, assert content == last acked watermark, byte-exact | 0 violations (200 examples/PR, 20k nightly) | Gate | PR (small) + nightly (deep) | P0, merge blocked; failing seed committed as regression case |
| 2 | pickle-free | "NO pickle anywhere" promise (DESIGN: codec; ROADMAP item 1) | `ast` walk over `src/datacrystal/**`: forbid `import`/`importlib` of pickle, dill, cloudpickle, shelve, joblib; forbid `marshal.dumps/loads`, `copyreg.pickle`; format fixtures scanned for pickle protocol magic bytes | 0 findings | Gate | every PR | merge blocked |
| 3 | dep-budget / import-isolation | Core deps = msgspec + pyroaring only; extras stay extras (ROADMAP; DESIGN amendment; SDA hard boundaries) | (a) assert `[project.dependencies]` ŌŖå {msgspec, pyroaring}; (b) fresh subprocess `import datacrystal`, assert `sys.modules` Ōł® {pyarrow, duckdb, polars, usearch, pydantic, numpy, psutil, fastapi, strawberry, sqlite3*} = Ōłģ (*sqlite3 lazily, only on store open) | 0 leaks | Gate | every PR | merge blocked |
| 4 | ADR-001 conformance | Owner-confinement Option D contract (ADR-001, all six bound sub-decisions) | executable spec suite: foreign-thread read/write/`Lazy.get()`/`commit()` ŌåÆ `WrongThreadError`; `submit()` returning live entity ŌåÆ `EntityEscapeError`; `snapshot()` read from N threads while owner mutates (no tearing, stable watermark); `aopen()` loop binding; `debug=True` keeps hooks armed; writes racing commit-P2 re-dirty into next commit | 100% pass | Gate | every PR | merge blocked; contract change requires ADR amendment |
| 5 | replay determinism | Commit-delta/watermark pipeline is a PUBLIC VERSIONED contract (ROADMAP item 3; SCALING tier 3) | scripted + hypothesis op sequences; run twice (incl. different `PYTHONHASHSEED`): assert byte-identical delta stream, identical store content hash; replay deltas into a reference consumer == direct store state; delta application idempotent (apply twice == once) | byte-identical; idempotent | Gate | every PR | merge blocked; pipeline version bump procedure triggered |
| 6 | golden-file compat | Every release opens stores written by ALL prior releases (DESIGN amendment 7; format hygiene) | `goldens/v0.N/` store dirs committed at each release tag; test opens each with current code, verifies content checksums + reference queries | all goldens open + verify | Gate | every PR (cheap) + full matrix at release | format break: ship migration path or revert; never silent |
| 7 | format-header lock | Versioned header with reserved sealed-flag/footer-offset/tid-watermark fields (ROADMAP item 5) | byte-level test: encode reference record, compare against checked-in hex fixture incl. version byte + reserved fields | exact bytes | Gate | every PR | change requires version increment + fixture bump + golden plan |
| 8 | single-writer lease | Lease lock file, loud error; `--workers 4` is the #1 foreseeable user error (ROADMAP item 2; SCALING tier 1) | spawn 2nd process opening same store dir ŌåÆ clean `LockHeldError` (no hang/corruption); kill holder + age lease ŌåÆ takeover succeeds; N simultaneous openers ŌåÆ exactly one wins | all 3 scenarios pass | Gate | every PR | merge blocked |
| 9 | O(delta) watermark apply | Sidecars apply deltas O(delta), never rebuild-the-world (SDA lesson: per-write FTS rebuild; ROADMAP item 3) | apply a fixed 100-record commit to reference consumer (bitmap index; FTS5 when it lands) against stores of 10┬│/10ŌüĄ/10ŌüČ entities; fit time vs store size | t(10ŌüČ)/t(10┬│) Ōēż 2├Ś | Gate (ratio) + Trend (absolute) | nightly | P1; consumer not certified on pipeline until fixed |
| 10 | boot O(checkpoint) | Startup O(checkpoint), not O(write history) (DESIGN amendment 5; ROADMAP punt 14 rationale) | stores with identical 10Ōü┤-object live set but 1├Ś/10├Ś/100├Ś update churn; measure `open()` time | t(100├Ś)/t(1├Ś) Ōēż 1.5├Ś | Gate (ratio) + Trend | nightly | P1 before next release |
| 11 | memory envelope | ~600 B/live registered object all-in; 1ŌĆō5M object positioning (DESIGN amendment 5) | load reference dataset (1M mixed entities, Lazy boundaries per docs); RSS delta + tracemalloc ├Ę object count | Ōēż 600 B/obj | Gate + Trend chart | nightly | regression bisected before release; docs envelope never widened silently |
| 12 | import-time budget | CLI/agent persona: scripts must start fast (DESIGN amendment 1) | p50 of 10 cold-subprocess `python -c "import datacrystal"` runs; `-X importtime` profile archived | Ōēż 100 ms CI runner AND Ōēż 1.3├Ś stored baseline | Gate + Trend | every PR (timing), nightly (profile) | merge blocked; lazy-import the offender |
| 13 | sidecar rebuildability | Indexes = rebuildable derived data, never txn participants (DESIGN search pillar; SCALING tier 3) | delete sidecar/index files, reopen, rebuild from graph; assert bitmap/FTS query results identical to incrementally-maintained version | exact equality | Gate | every PR | merge blocked |
| 14 | no-N+1 hydration | Batch hydration; "N+1 must never be the user's problem" (SDA delta 5) | counting wrapper on storage protocol: batch-load 500 entities ŌåÆ count read calls | Ōēż 4 storage reads | Gate | every PR | merge blocked |
| 15 | steady-state overhead | One-shot `__setattr__`; zero steady-state cost (DESIGN dirty tracking; risk 1) | pytest-benchmark: attr read on Clean entity and 2nd+ write on Dirty entity vs plain slots-dataclass | Ōēż 1.05├Ś plain dataclass | Gate (ratio) + Trend | nightly | P1; hook disarm logic audited |
| 16 | ergonomics gate | "hello-world in 4 lines"; public API only (DESIGN MVP; positioning) | README quickstart extracted (pytest-examples) and executed verbatim in clean venv with core-only install; `examples/quickstart.py` Ōēż 60 lines, AST check: imports only public `datacrystal` names (no `_internal`) | runs green; Ōēż 60 lines | Gate | every PR | merge blocked |
| 17 | wheel purity | Pure-Python-first; Never-list: no Rust in core (ROADMAP Never) | `uv build`; assert wheel tag `py3-none-any`, no `.so`/`.pyd` members | pure wheel | Gate | every PR + release | merge blocked |

## 2. The M0 five (before any engine code lands)

1. **#2 pickle-free** ŌĆö the no-pickle promise is unrecoverable once the first format byte ships; trivially green at zero code, contamination-proof from commit one.
2. **#3 dep-budget / import-isolation** ŌĆö dependency creep is a one-way door; the budget must be enforced before the first convenient `import numpy` appears.
3. **#1 crash-torture harness** ŌĆö the buffer-until-commit storer must be *born inside* this harness (DESIGN: "from day one"); the fault-injection wrapper also forces the 3-method storage protocol to exist first.
4. **#4 ADR-001 conformance suite** ŌĆö written as the executable spec of the accepted contract, so the engine API is shaped by failing tests instead of retrofitted guards.
5. **#5 replay determinism / watermark contract** ŌĆö ROADMAP: semantics "locked before any consumer ships"; the most load-bearing undelivered component gets its contract test before its implementation.

## 3. How they run

**Layout:** all fitness functions live in `tests/fitness/` as pytest (static AST checks included, so one runner does everything), markers `gate`, `trend`, `slow`; shared fault-injection harness (`FaultyStorage`, child-process runner) in `tests/fitness/_harness.py` ŌĆö deliberately reusable by extension packages as pipeline-consumer certification.

**Local:**
- `uv run pytest tests/fitness -m gate` ŌĆö full PR-gate set (#1-small, 2ŌĆō8, 12ŌĆō14, 16, 17), target < 3 min.
- `uv run pytest tests/fitness -m "slow or trend" --hypothesis-profile=nightly` ŌĆö deep torture (20k examples), perf fits, memory envelope.
- `uv run python -X importtime -c "import datacrystal"` ŌĆö ad-hoc import budget drill-down.

**CI (GitHub Actions):**
- `pr.yml` (ubuntu + macos, py3.14): the `-m gate` set. Cheap on every PR: #2, 3, 6, 7, 8, 12, 13, 14, 16, 17 are sub-second-to-seconds; #1 runs the 200-example profile; #4, 5 are plain unit-speed suites.
- `nightly.yml` (cron): #1 deep, #9, 10, 11, 15; perf checks **gate on size-scaling ratios within one run** (slope/ratio, machine-independent) and only **trend** absolute wall times ŌĆö results appended to `benchmarks/history.jsonl`, alert at Ōēź 20% drift vs 7-day median. Avoids flaky absolute thresholds on shared runners.
- `release.yml` (tag): full golden matrix across all prior `goldens/v0.*`, then writes + commits the new release's golden store; wheel-purity audit; publish blocked until PyPI names `datacrystal`/`data-crystal` are reserved.

Threshold changes to any gate require a PR touching both the threshold and the doc it cites ŌĆö fitness functions are part of the public contract surface, versioned with it.

fitnessCritique.summary: The catalog is fundamentally sound ŌĆö the M0-five sequencing is correct, the gate/trend split mostly follows its own ratio-not-absolute principle, and the Never list and dep boundaries are well covered. But under adversarial reading, several perf gates are mis-instrumented as written (timing nanosecond/microsecond operations against 5%-2x thresholds, tracemalloc contaminating the RSS it measures, an absolute 100ms wall-clock PR gate that violates the doc's own anti-flakiness rule), the ADR-001 suite claims all six bound sub-decisions while silently omitting the daemon principle (race c), and the versioning promises are tested in one direction only ŌĆö old-code-opens-newer-store (DESIGN amendment 7) and sidecar-ahead-of-core detection (amendment 6) have no fitness function at all.

defects.defect: #4 ADR-001 conformance claims to cover 'all six bound sub-decisions' but the mechanism lists no check for sub-decision 3, the daemon principle ŌĆö 'the LazyReferenceManager NEVER touches the live graph from a foreign thread'. Race (c) (use-after-clear during demotion) is one of the five races ADR-001 exists to close and explicitly 'bites even single-threaded apps', yet nothing in the suite exercises demotion at all. The gate goes green while the single most subtle contract clause erodes.
defects.fix: Add to the conformance suite: instrument the ghost-demotion path to record the acting thread/task id and assert it is always the owner (or the owner-side piggyback) in both sync and async modes; plus a stress case where the manager's timeout/RSS trigger fires while the owner reads the same entity under debug=True, asserting no use-after-clear.

defects.defect: #12 import-time budget gates every PR on 'Ōēż 100 ms CI runner' ŌĆö an absolute wall-clock threshold on shared GitHub runners, the exact flaky-by-construction pattern Section 3 itself bans for nightly perf ('avoids flaky absolute thresholds on shared runners'). The 'Ōēż 1.3├Ś stored baseline' half compares against a measurement from a different run/machine, which is the same cross-machine flakiness with extra steps.
defects.fix: Make the PR gate a same-run ratio: measure cold-subprocess import of datacrystal vs a fixed reference import (e.g. `import msgspec` or bare interpreter startup) in the same job and gate on that ratio; keep the 100 ms number as a nightly trend alert on a pinned runner class, and archive -X importtime profiles for the trend as already specified.

defects.defect: #15 steady-state overhead gates at Ōēż 1.05├Ś plain dataclass for attribute reads/writes ŌĆö a 5% threshold on ~20-50 ns operations. Even within one pytest-benchmark run, ns-scale comparisons swing more than 5% from CPU frequency scaling, core migration, and cache state on shared runners; this gate will cry wolf and the threshold will get quietly widened, after which it guards nothing. The actual ratified property ('zero steady-state cost, one-shot __setattr__') is structural, not statistical.
defects.fix: Replace the primary gate with deterministic structural assertions: after the one-shot hook fires/disarms, assert `type(entity).__setattr__` is the original slot descriptor path and Clean-entity `__getattribute__` is unhooked (identity comparison against the plain class's attributes); keep the pytest-benchmark numbers as nightly Trend with a looser (e.g. 1.25├Ś) alert, not a Gate.

defects.defect: #11 memory envelope measures 'RSS delta + tracemalloc' in the same run. Enabling tracemalloc allocates trace bookkeeping for every allocation (tens of bytes per object at 1M objects), so the RSS delta it reports includes tracemalloc's own overhead ŌĆö the 600 B/obj gate is contaminated by its measurement instrument and will either fail spuriously or get widened to absorb the instrument, silently breaking the 'envelope never widened silently' promise. Allocator/platform variance (ubuntu glibc vs macOS) also makes one absolute byte threshold wrong on at least one OS.
defects.fix: Run two separate child processes: one plain run measuring RSS delta only (the gating number, with per-platform baselines), one tracemalloc run used only for attribution/bisection when the gate fails. Gate per-platform against checked-in baselines with a ratio (e.g. Ōēż 1.1├Ś baseline) plus the absolute 600 B as the documented ceiling on the reference platform.

defects.defect: #9 O(delta) gate times a fixed 100-record apply ŌĆö microseconds for bitmap deltas, low milliseconds for FTS5 ŌĆö and gates on the ratio of two tiny noisy wall-clock numbers (t(10ŌüČ)/t(10┬│) Ōēż 2├Ś). Ratio framing does not rescue measurements where scheduler jitter exceeds the signal; this is flaky-by-construction at the stated threshold. Separately, rebuilding 10ŌüČ-entity stores from scratch every night is the expensive part of the check while contributing nothing to the property under test.
defects.fix: Gate on operation counts, not time: the catalog already builds a counting storage wrapper for #14 ŌĆö assert the number of storage reads/writes/rows touched during apply is a function of delta size only (identical across 10┬│/10ŌüĄ/10ŌüČ stores, or bounded by delta┬Ęlog n with explicit allowance). Keep wall-clock as Trend only. Pre-build the three reference stores once and cache them as versioned CI artifacts (regenerate on format-version bump).

defects.defect: #1 crash-torture's regression mechanism is partially unreproducible as described: 'real SIGKILL at randomized commit-phase boundaries' depends on OS scheduling, so 'failing seed committed as regression case' cannot replay the SIGKILL timing from a hypothesis seed. Also 'assert content == last acked watermark, byte-exact' is undefined for the SQLite-as-blob backend ŌĆö post-recovery file bytes legitimately differ (WAL/journal/freelist state) from any canonical form, so byte-comparing the store is wrong and comparing 'content' is unspecified. Third, 200 examples each spawning a child process + store open/close threatens the < 3 min full-gate budget on macOS runners by itself.
defects.fix: Make the fault plan explicit hypothesis-generated data (op index, commit phase, byte offset, fault type) that the child self-triggers via the FaultyStorage hook and os._exit ŌĆö fully seed-reproducible and shrinkable; keep real SIGKILL as a nightly smoke layer without seed claims. Define the assertion as logical-state equality: decode all entities after reopen and compare against a shadow model at the last acked watermark (byte-exact on the re-encoded msgspec records, never on the SQLite file). Time-box the PR profile (hypothesis deadline / batch several examples per child process) and verify the 3-min budget in CI.

defects.defect: Coverage gap ŌĆö versioning is tested in one direction only. #6 goldens prove new code opens old stores, but DESIGN amendment 7 ratifies 'defined behavior for old-code-opens-newer-store' and nothing checks it; similarly the commit-delta pipeline is a 'public versioned contract' (ROADMAP item 3) but no check asserts that a consumer encountering a newer pipeline version fails cleanly rather than misreading deltas. Both are exactly the silent-format-break failure modes the catalog claims to guard.
defects.fix: Add to release.yml: in a clean venv, `pip install datacrystal==<previous release>` and open the NEW release's golden store, asserting the documented behavior (loud versioned error or defined read-only mode per amendment 7); and feed a delta stream with an incremented pipeline-version field to the previous reference consumer, asserting clean rejection. Cheap PR-cadence variant: current code opening a fixture with version byte = current+1.

defects.defect: Coverage gap ŌĆö DESIGN amendment 6's 'sidecar-ahead-of-core detection forces index rebuild' has no fitness function. #13 tests delete-sidecar-and-rebuild (sidecar behind/missing), but the dangerous direction is the opposite: core restored from backup (the documented Litestream/sqlite3.backup PITR recipe in ROADMAP item 6 makes this a first-class workflow) while the sidecar watermark is ahead ŌĆö an undetected stale-ahead sidecar serves wrong query results indefinitely.
defects.fix: Add a scenario to #13: snapshot the store dir, advance N commits (sidecar applies them), restore the older core files under the newer sidecar, reopen ŌĆö assert the watermark regression is detected, a rebuild is forced, and post-rebuild query results equal a from-scratch rebuild.

defects.defect: #7 format-header lock is gameable exactly where it matters: the test only compares current encoding against the checked-in fixture, so a PR that changes the header layout AND regenerates the fixture passes green with the version byte unchanged. The 'change requires version increment' rule is process prose, not mechanism ŌĆö the gate cannot see that the fixture moved without a version bump.
defects.fix: Check in an append-only registry mapping format-version byte ŌåÆ fixture content hash; the test asserts (a) current encoding matches the fixture, (b) the fixture hash for every previously-registered version is unchanged, (c) a new/changed fixture requires a new version entry. Changing bytes under an existing version then fails mechanically regardless of fixture regeneration.

defects.defect: #2's 'format fixtures scanned for pickle protocol magic bytes' will false-positive on legitimate msgpack: pickle's magic is just b'\x80' + a protocol byte, and 0x80 is msgpack's fixmap(0) (any empty dict field), with 0x02-0x05 being common positive fixints right after. A two-byte scan over arbitrary msgpack streams is noise, and the resulting spurious red gates will train people to ignore or weaken the check.
defects.fix: Restrict the magic-byte scan to record-body offsets where a pickle payload could actually start (record start / blob field start), or replace it with a positive check: every fixture record must decode cleanly under the strict msgspec schema (validating decode is already 'structurally incapable of code execution' ŌĆö decodability under the whitelist is the stronger property). Keep the AST import ban as the primary mechanism.

defects.defect: #8 has a measurability problem and a missing scenario. 'Kill holder + age lease' requires either a real wall-clock wait of the lease TTL inside the PR gate or an unstated injectable clock. And the nastier failure mode is untested: a paused holder (GC pause, laptop sleep ŌĆö core persona is desktop) whose lease expires and is taken over, then resumes and keeps writing ŌĆö silent two-writer corruption, the exact thing the lease exists to prevent.
defects.fix: Make lease TTL and clock injectable in the test config (sub-second TTL for the PR gate, no sleeps near real TTL). Add scenario 4: suspend the holder (SIGSTOP), let a second process take over after expiry, SIGCONT the original ŌĆö assert its next refresh/write raises a loud lease-lost error (the EclipseStore StorageLockFileManager self-check being ported) and the store is uncorrupted.

defects.defect: #10 boot O(checkpoint) is near-vacuous for its entire stated lifetime: ROADMAP punt 14 says 'SQLite-blob v0.x has no boot problem ŌĆö boot index *is* the B-tree', and with newest-wins one-row-per-OID storage the 1├Ś/10├Ś/100├Ś churn stores are structurally identical on disk, so the ratio gate tests SQLite's B-tree, not datacrystal. Meanwhile it burns nightly budget generating ~10ŌüČ update operations, and the component it actually guards (custom append-log boot chain) is punted post-v1.
defects.fix: Replace the timing mechanism with a counting-wrapper assertion that does guard something now: open() must issue O(live-set) storage reads independent of total historical write count and dead-version rows (catches anyone accidentally introducing a full-history scan or version-keeping rows). Park the churn-ratio timing variant in the harness, activated as a gate only when ROADMAP item 14 (custom log) is picked up; until then drop it from nightly.
